ollama_format.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
ollama_format.title: StructuredSmokeText

structured_output_schema.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
structured_output_schema.title: StructuredSmokeText

parsed_response.response_text: The count of households with E-RO behavioral type begins at 20 households across all time steps, remaining constant for the initial 20 steps. This suggests strong stability in E-RO strategy adoption under homogeneous conditions. At time step 21, variability emerges, with counts oscillating between 13 and 22 households. A significant decline occurs around time step 80, where many simulation runs show near-zero household counts (e.g., 0 households in multiple runs). By time step 100, restored stability is observed, with 20 households consistently adopting the E-RO strategy across all runs. Recovery phases appear between steps 70-85, where counts briefly rebound from low levels. The persistent fluctuations between 0-20 households in early stages highlight competition or stress effects under fixed learning constraints. The model’s default homogeneity parameter (homog-behav-types? = True) forces initial E-RO dominance, while later declines may reflect resource depletion dynamics in competitive grazing systems with strict non-learning behavior.
